schema: CREATE TABLE employees (id int, name text, salary int, department text);
question: What are the names of employees in the 'Sales' department?
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales'

schema: CREATE TABLE employees (id int, name text, salary int, department text);
question: How many employees earn more than 50000?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000

schema: CREATE TABLE projects (id int, name text, budget int, status text);
question: List all project names with a budget over 100000.
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM projects WHERE budget > 100000

schema: CREATE TABLE projects (id int, name text, budget int, status text);
question: Show the status of the 'Website Redesign' project.
expected_sql: SELECT status FROM projects WHERE name = 'Website Redesign'

schema: CREATE TABLE employees (id int, name text, salary int, department text);
question: Show the average salary of all employees.
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees

schema: CREATE TABLE doctors (id int, name text, dept_id int); CREATE TABLE departments (id int, name text);
question: List each department name and the number of doctors working there.
expected_sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM doctors AS T1 JOIN departments AS T2 ON T1.dept_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name

schema: CREATE TABLE patients (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE appointments (id int, patient_id int, date text);
question: Show the names of patients who have more than 2 appointments.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM patients AS T1 JOIN appointments AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.patient_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.id) > 2

schema: CREATE TABLE singer (id int, name text, age int); CREATE TABLE concert (id int, name text, year int); CREATE TABLE singer_in_concert (singer_id int, concert_id int);
question: Find the names of singers who have performed in at least 2 concerts.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.singer_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING COUNT(T2.concert_id) >= 2

schema: CREATE TABLE car_makers (id int, maker text); CREATE TABLE model_list (model_id int, maker int, model text);
question: Show all car makers and the count of models they produce.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.maker, COUNT(T2.model_id) FROM car_makers AS T1 JOIN model_list AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.maker GROUP BY T1.maker

schema: CREATE TABLE airlines (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE flights (id int, airline int, source text);
question: Show the name of the airline that has the most flights starting from 'JFK'.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airline WHERE T2.source = 'JFK' GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

schema: CREATE TABLE employees (id int, name text, salary int, dept_id int); CREATE TABLE departments (id int, name text, budget int);
question: Find the names of employees working in departments with a budget greater than 500000.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM employees AS T1 JOIN departments AS T2 ON T1.dept_id = T2.id WHERE T2.budget > 500000

schema: CREATE TABLE products (id int, name text, price int); CREATE TABLE sales (id int, product_id int, quantity int);
question: What is the total revenue generate by the product 'iPhone'?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(T1.price * T2.quantity) FROM products AS T1 JOIN sales AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.name = 'iPhone'

schema: CREATE TABLE students (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE grades (student_id int, course_id int, grade int);
question: List students who have an average grade above 85.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM students AS T1 JOIN grades AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING AVG(T2.grade) > 85

schema: CREATE TABLE books (id int, title text, author_id int); CREATE TABLE authors (id int, name text);
question: How many books has each author written? List author name and count.
expected_sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM books AS T1 JOIN authors AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name

schema: CREATE TABLE customers (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE accounts (id int, customer_id int, balance int);
question: Find customers who have a total balance across all accounts greater than 10000.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM customers AS T1 JOIN accounts AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.name HAVING SUM(T2.balance) > 10000

schema: CREATE TABLE singer (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE concert (id int, name text, year int); CREATE TABLE singer_in_concert (singer_id int, concert_id int);
question: List the names of singers who have performed in both 2024 and 2025 concerts.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.singer_id JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_id = T3.id WHERE T3.year = 2024 INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM singer AS T1 JOIN singer_in_concert AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.singer_id JOIN concert AS T3 ON T2.concert_id = T3.id WHERE T3.year = 2025

schema: CREATE TABLE physician (id int, name text); CREATE TABLE department (id int, name text, head int);
question: What are the names of physicians who are not heads of any department?
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM physician WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT head FROM department)

schema: CREATE TABLE model_list (model_id int, maker int, model text); CREATE TABLE car_makers (id int, maker text, country int); CREATE TABLE countries (id int, continent int);
question: List the models of cars produced by makers whose countries are in continent 1.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.model FROM model_list AS T1 JOIN car_makers AS T2 ON T1.maker = T2.id JOIN countries AS T3 ON T2.country = T3.id WHERE T3.continent = 1

schema: CREATE TABLE airlines (id int, name text, country text); CREATE TABLE flights (id int, airline int, source text, dest text);
question: Show airline names that have flights from 'JFK' but NO flights to 'LAX'.
expected_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airline WHERE T2.source = 'JFK' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.airline WHERE T3.dest = 'LAX'

schema: CREATE TABLE instructor (id int, name text, dept_name text); CREATE TABLE department (dept_name text, budget int);
question: Find the names of all instructors who work in a department with a budget higher than the average department budget.
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM instructor WHERE dept_name IN (SELECT dept_name FROM department WHERE budget > (SELECT AVG(budget) FROM department))
